 {"id":"bd-2ad","title":"Implement FUSE mount lifecycle (mount/unmount/signal handling)","description":"# Implement FUSE mount lifecycle (mount/unmount/signal handling)

## GOAL
Implement robust mount lifecycle management with clean startup, shutdown, and signal handling.

## BACKGROUND
Production filesystems must handle:
- Clean mount with validation
- Graceful unmount with flush
- Signal handling (SIGTERM, SIGINT)
- Crash recovery on restart

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Mount Phase
```rust
pub async fn mount(config: MountConfig, cx: &Cx) -> Result<MountHandle> {
    // 1. Validate image (superblock, journal state)
    // 2. Replay journal if needed (ext4)
    // 3. Initialize MVCC store
    // 4. Initialize ARC cache
    // 5. Initialize repair manager
    // 6. Start background tasks (scrub, GC)
    // 7. Mount FUSE session
    // 8. Return handle for lifecycle control
}
```

### 2. Unmount Phase
```rust
impl MountHandle {
    pub async fn unmount(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<()> {
        // 1. Stop accepting new requests
        // 2. Wait for in-flight requests (with timeout)
        // 3. Flush dirty cache entries
        // 4. Commit pending transactions
        // 5. Refresh repair symbols for dirty groups
        // 6. Close FUSE session
        // 7. Sync and close device
    }
}
```

### 3. Signal Handling
- SIGTERM/SIGINT: Trigger graceful unmount
- SIGUSR1: Dump debug state to log
- Timeout for unmount (default 30s, then force)

### 4. Drop Safety
- MountHandle::drop must trigger unmount
- Panic in unmount must not corrupt filesystem
- Leaked handle must eventually timeout

## TESTS
1. Unit: Mount/unmount roundtrip
2. Integration: SIGTERM during I/O results in clean state
3. Integration: Double-mount same image fails cleanly
4. Stress: Mount/unmount loop 100x without leaks

## LOGGING
- Mount: log config, image path, mount point
- Unmount: log flush stats, pending ops
- Signal: log signal received, action taken

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `ffs mount ... && ffs umount ...` leaves clean state
2. `kill -TERM $(pidof ffs)` results in clean unmount
3. Remount after crash shows no corruption
4. No resource leaks (file descriptors, memory)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:50:34.171965858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T10:14:20.310382109Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T10:14:20.310314994Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse","lifecycle","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ad","depends_on_id":"bd-20p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:06.032850244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ad","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:37.263209229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":26,"issue_id":"bd-2ad","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FEATURE_PARITY mapping: This bead covers the 'FUSE mount runtime' row (currently ❌). When complete, flip to ✅ and update ParityReport::current() counts.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:49Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2c1y","title":"MVCC durability: persist WAL before exposing commit","description":"Context: current PersistentMvccStore commit flow applies to in-memory store before WAL append, creating crash window where acknowledged commit is lost on replay.\n\nScope:\n- Reorder commit pipeline so WAL append/sync succeeds before in-memory visibility of commit.\n- Ensure WAL append failure returns error and does not expose transaction versions.\n- Add regression tests for WAL failure path and replay durability.\n\nAcceptance:\n- commit() does not mutate visible store state when WAL append fails.\n- successful commit remains replayable after restart.\n- targeted tests pass via rch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-19T02:07:05.755076103Z","created_by":"SilverLake","updated_at":"2026-02-19T02:21:03.539117230Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T02:21:03.539092574Z","close_reason":"Completed: WAL failure now rolls back in-memory commit state; mvcc durability tests and workspace gates pass via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","durability","mvcc","wal"]}

 {"id":"bd-4ogh","title":"Implement ext4/btrfs parity features: decompression, inline data, bigalloc, ZERO_RANGE","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T18:28:47.537528441Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T18:29:02.634096851Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T18:29:02.633525312Z","close_reason":"Implemented 4 new parity features:
(1) btrfs transparent decompression: ZLIB (flate2) + ZSTD in read path for inline and regular extents. Added ram_bytes field to BtrfsExtentData enum. LZO deferred (custom framing).
(2) ext4 inline data read: reads from inode extent_bytes + xattr_ibody when EXT4_INLINE_DATA_FL is set. Removed INLINE_DATA from REJECTED_V1 incompat set.
(3) ext4 bigalloc infrastructure: added cluster_ratio to FsGeometry (cluster_size/block_size for BIGALLOC, else 1).
(4) ext4 ZERO_RANGE fallocate: writes zero blocks to existing allocated extents in range. Mode 0x10 now accepted.
All tests pass (471 ffs-core, 360 ffs-ondisk, 142 ffs-alloc, 117 ffs-btrfs, 114 ffs-extent).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ext4","parity"]}

 {"id":"bd-4y8f","title":"Add btrfs kernel_reference golden tests matching ext4 coverage pattern","description":"## What
Add structured golden-fixture conformance tests for btrfs metadata parsing, matching the
ext4 kernel_reference.rs pattern of: create real image → parse → compare to golden JSON.

## Prior Art (what already exists)
- ext4 has crates/ffs-harness/tests/kernel_reference.rs with multiple test variants:
  creates real mkfs.ext4 images, parses them, validates against golden JSON fixtures
- btrfs E2E tests exist in fuse_e2e.rs (lines 1889-1910) that create mkfs.btrfs images
  and mount them via FUSE
- btrfs conformance fixtures in conformance/fixtures/ are synthetic JSON (not from real images)
- ffs-core unit tests build synthetic btrfs images in-memory (build_btrfs_fsops_image)

## The Specific Gap
btrfs has no equivalent of kernel_reference.rs that:
1. Creates a real btrfs filesystem with known content
2. Parses the superblock, tree structures, and metadata with FrankenFS
3. Compares the parsed output against committed golden JSON fixtures
4. Catches regressions when parsing code changes

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1: Create crates/ffs-harness/tests/btrfs_kernel_reference.rs

fn btrfs_golden_basic_superblock()
  - mkfs.btrfs -f --nodesize 16384 on 256MB sparse file
  - Parse superblock with BtrfsSuperblock::parse_superblock_region()
  - Compare: magic, sectorsize, nodesize, total_bytes, num_devices, csum_type
  - Golden: conformance/fixtures/btrfs_basic_superblock.json

fn btrfs_golden_with_files()
  - mkfs.btrfs → mount → create 3 test files with known content → umount
  - Parse: walk root tree → fs tree → verify inode items, dir items, extent data
  - Golden: conformance/fixtures/btrfs_with_files.json

fn btrfs_golden_with_compression()
  - mkfs.btrfs → mount -o compress=zlib → create files → umount
  - Parse: verify EXTENT_DATA items have compression type set
  - Golden: conformance/fixtures/btrfs_compressed.json

fn btrfs_golden_with_subvolume()
  - mkfs.btrfs → mount → btrfs subvolume create → add files → umount
  - Parse: verify ROOT_ITEM for subvolume, walk subvol tree
  - Golden: conformance/fixtures/btrfs_subvolume.json

fn btrfs_golden_chunk_and_device_trees()
  - mkfs.btrfs → parse chunk tree entries → verify stripe mapping
  - Golden: conformance/fixtures/btrfs_chunk_tree.json

### Step 2: Create golden fixture files
- Run tests once with REGEN_GOLDENS=1 to generate initial fixtures
- Commit fixtures to conformance/fixtures/btrfs_*.json
- Subsequent runs compare against committed fixtures

### Step 3: Add regeneration support
- scripts/regen_btrfs_goldens.sh: runs tests with REGEN_GOLDENS=1
- Similar to existing scripts/verify_golden.sh for ext4

## Considerations
- mkfs.btrfs minimum image: ~128MB for single-device, use sparse files
- btrfs-progs required: mkfs.btrfs, btrfs (for subvolume create, property set)
- Tests must skip gracefully when btrfs-progs not installed
- Some features (multi-device RAID) need multiple loop devices — skip in CI,
  add as a separate optional test
- Golden format: pretty-printed JSON matching the ext4 golden style
- Mount operations need root or fusermount3 — use btrfs-progs direct manipulation
  where possible (debugfs equivalent: btrfs inspect-internal dump-tree)

## Test Plan
- Each golden test: parse real image → compare to fixture → assert exact match
- Regeneration: REGEN_GOLDENS=1 cargo test -p ffs-harness --test btrfs_kernel_reference
- Verify: changing parsing code causes golden mismatch (regression detection works)
- Skip tests gracefully when mkfs.btrfs unavailable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 5 btrfs golden conformance tests implemented and passing
- [ ] Golden JSON fixtures committed to conformance/fixtures/
- [ ] Tests create real mkfs.btrfs images (not synthetic)
- [ ] Regression detection verified: intentional parse change → test failure
- [ ] Tests skip gracefully when btrfs-progs unavailable
- [ ] Regeneration script works","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T15:49:25.746184725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:05:11.260412024Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T23:05:11.260211284Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-sqc5g","title":"add mounted-path ext4 hard-link refusal coverage for occupied destination EEXIST and non-directory parent ENOTDIR","description":"Survey finding: ext4 mounted-path FUSE E2E proves hard-link success plus some refusal paths, but it does not freeze the operator-visible errors for linking onto an occupied destination or into a non-directory parent even though ffs-core already enforces EEXIST and ENOTDIR. Scope: add ext4 mounted-path probes that assert hard_link over an existing destination returns EEXIST and hard_link into a regular-file parent returns ENOTDIR, with unchanged nlink and directory contents. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes ext4 hard-link refusal behavior for occupied-destination and non-directory-parent cases.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:09:23.154185107Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:11:49.540633974Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-sqtrq","title":"stress fuse inode lock ordering under concurrent requests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:34:13.423718762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T01:22:03.963392029Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T01:22:03.960982426Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-sv1b2","title":"btrfs core: resolve named subvolume and snapshot mount selections","description":"Survey finding: `FEATURE_PARITY.md` and `README.md` both claim btrfs subvolume mount support, but `OpenFs::from_device` still hardcodes `BTRFS_FS_TREE_OBJECTID` and the operator-facing CLI rejects `--subvol/--snapshot`. This bead owns the core selection logic in `ffs-core`.

Scope:
- Extend `OpenOptions` with an explicit btrfs mount selection contract (default root, named subvolume, named snapshot, and optionally numeric objectid if that simplifies diagnostics).
- Walk the root tree and resolve the requested selection to the mounted `subvol_objectid` plus `subvol_root_dirid`.
- Return deterministic `FfsError` variants for missing selections, ambiguous names, and selection/type mismatches.
- Keep default-root behavior unchanged when no selection is requested.

Acceptance:
- `OpenFs::open_with_options` can mount a non-default subvolume/snapshot without post-open mutation hacks.
- Synthetic and/or kernel-reference tests prove correct resolution for default root, named subvolume, named snapshot, and missing target cases.
- Structured diagnostics include the requested selector and resolved objectid/root dirid.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:51:50.465599701Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:51:50.462982467Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","mount","parity"]}
-{"id":"bd-sv2c3","title":"ffs-cli: wire btrfs --subvol and --snapshot mount flags to OpenOptions","description":"Survey finding: `ffs-cli` advertises `--subvol`/`--snapshot` on `mount`, but `validate_btrfs_mount_selection()` hard-rejects both flags even though the underlying codebase already has internal btrfs subvolume context machinery. This bead owns the operator-facing CLI plumbing.

Scope:
- Remove the unconditional rejection path in `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs`.
- Thread the requested selection into `OpenOptions` and preserve all existing ext4 behavior.
- Emit structured startup/rejection logs that show requested selector, resolved selection type, and failure class.
- Replace the current `mount_rejects_subvol_flag` / `mount_rejects_snapshot_flag` tests with positive routing tests and deterministic failure diagnostics for nonexistent selectors.

Acceptance:
- `ffs mount ... --subvol <name>` and `ffs mount ... --snapshot <name>` reach `OpenFs::open_with_options` instead of bailing in argument validation.
- Missing selectors fail with clear, stable user-facing errors.
- CLI tests cover default-root, explicit subvolume, explicit snapshot, and nonexistent selection cases.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:51:58.844877638Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","cli","mount"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sv2c3","depends_on_id":"bd-sv1b2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-sv3d4","title":"add operator-facing coverage and parity evidence for btrfs subvolume/snapshot mount","description":"Survey finding: current conformance only proves the default btrfs root-alias path, while docs/parity wording imply user-selectable subvolume mount support. This bead closes that evidence gap after the core and CLI work land.

Scope:
- Add harness and/or FUSE E2E coverage that mounts a non-default btrfs subvolume and snapshot through the real CLI/operator path.
- Assert mounted-root `getattr`/`lookup`/`readdir`/`read` behavior is scoped to the selected tree, not the default root.
- Update `FEATURE_PARITY.md` and README wording so the operator-facing support claim matches the tested surface exactly.
- Ensure test output/logs emit machine-parseable scenario identifiers for success and failure cases.

Acceptance:
- There is deterministic coverage for mounting a named subvolume and a named snapshot via the public mount path.
- README and `FEATURE_PARITY.md` no longer overclaim relative to the tested operator surface.
- Coverage artifacts make it clear whether support is internal-only or operator-visible.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:26:00.725654945Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:26:00.725654945Z","close_reason":"Added CLI operator-path named subvolume/snapshot mount coverage and aligned parity/docs wording","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","docs","e2e","mount","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sv3d4","depends_on_id":"bd-sv1b2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-sv3d4","depends_on_id":"bd-sv2c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"bd-sv3d4","title":"add operator-facing coverage and parity evidence for btrfs subvolume/snapshot mount","description":"Survey finding: current conformance only proves the default btrfs root-alias path, while docs/parity wording imply user-selectable subvolume mount support. This bead closes that evidence gap after the core and CLI work land.

Scope:
- Add harness and/or FUSE E2E coverage that mounts a non-default btrfs subvolume and snapshot through the real CLI/operator path.
- Assert mounted-root `getattr`/`lookup`/`readdir`/`read` behavior is scoped to the selected tree, not the default root.
- Update `FEATURE_PARITY.md` and README wording so the operator-facing support claim matches the tested surface exactly.
- Ensure test output/logs emit machine-parseable scenario identifiers for success and failure cases.

Acceptance:
- There is deterministic coverage for mounting a named subvolume and a named snapshot via the public mount path.
- README and `FEATURE_PARITY.md` no longer overclaim relative to the tested operator surface.
- Coverage artifacts make it clear whether support is internal-only or operator-visible.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:01:57.367032203Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:01:57.364778029Z","close_reason":"Added FUSE E2E tests for BtrfsMountSelection (subvolume/snapshot mounting). @@ -61,7 +61,7 @@
 | ext4 fast commit replay | `fs/ext4/fast_commit.c` | ✅ | `replay_fast_commit()` in `ffs-journal` parses FC tag streams (HEAD/TAIL/INODE/ADD_RANGE/DEL_RANGE/CREAT/LINK/UNLINK/PAD), buffers operations until commit `TAIL`, and forces fallback when the stream is truncated or incomplete. `OpenFs` extracts deterministic fast-commit evidence and applies committed FC operations at mount time via `apply_fast_commit_operations()`. FC operations are processed after JBD2 replay: Create/Link/Unlink/AddRange/DelRange are logged as observational evidence (JBD2 replay provides the authoritative block-level recovery); InodeUpdate triggers a verification read to confirm the inode is accessible. Truncated or incomplete FC streams fall back to JBD2-only replay. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies committed fast-commit evidence extraction plus deterministic JBD2-only fallback for truncated FC streams, and `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_ext4_fast_commit.rs` now coverage-fuzzes the raw fast-commit replay parser with golden-derived committed and truncated seeds. |
 | btrfs tree-log replay | `fs/btrfs/tree-log.c` | ✅ | `replay_tree_log()` in ffs-btrfs walks tree-log tree when `log_root != 0`, returns items for FS tree merge, and is wired into the mount path. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies multilevel log-tree replay through chunk mapping plus the zero-`log_root` fast path, and `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_btrfs_tree_log.rs` now coverage-fuzzes synthesized tree-log recovery over valid multilevel trees, absent log roots, checksum/structure failures, and equivalent chunk mappings. |
 | ext4 casefold (case-insensitive dirs) | `fs/ext4/namei.c` | ✅ | `lookup_in_dir_block_casefold()` with Unicode lowercase comparison. CASEFOLD removed from rejected features. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies real `OpenFs` lookup/readdir behavior on a CASEFOLD-flagged ext4 directory image. |
-| ext4 fscrypt (nokey read-only mode) | `fs/ext4/crypto.c` | ✅ | ENCRYPT removed from rejected features. Encrypted filenames shown as raw bytes (nokey mode). Full decryption requires key management not in V1. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies raw-byte `readdir`/`lookup` on an `ENCRYPT`-flagged ext4 image. `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs` now unit-tests v1 and v2 fscrypt context extraction for `get_encryption_policy_ex()`, plus `ENODATA` on unencrypted inodes. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit-tests both the legacy `_IOW` request shape for `FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY` and the `_IOWR` `FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX` path, including v1 encoding, v2-context passthrough, short-buffer rejection, and `ENODATA` on unencrypted inodes. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` verifies mounted-path `ENODATA` for an unencrypted inode and makes the current restricted-FUSE legacy-v1 success gap auditable: the request reaches `ffs-fuse::ioctl`, but current kernels surface `EIO` because the legacy getter advertises `out_size == 0`. |
+| ext4 fscrypt (nokey read-only mode) | `fs/ext4/crypto.c` | ✅ | ENCRYPT removed from rejected features. Encrypted filenames shown as raw bytes (nokey mode). Full decryption requires key management not in V1. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` verifies raw-byte `readdir`/`lookup` on an `ENCRYPT`-flagged ext4 image, and `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now freezes the same operator-visible mounted-path contract for raw-byte `readdir` plus path lookup. `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs` now unit-tests v1 and v2 fscrypt context extraction for `get_encryption_policy_ex()`, plus `ENODATA` on unencrypted inodes. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit-tests both the legacy `_IOW` request shape for `FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY` and the `_IOWR` `FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX` path, including v1 encoding, v2-context passthrough, short-buffer rejection, and `ENODATA` on unencrypted inodes. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` also verifies mounted-path `ENODATA` for an unencrypted inode and makes the current restricted-FUSE legacy-v1 success gap auditable: the request reaches `ffs-fuse::ioctl`, but current kernels surface `EIO` because the legacy getter advertises `out_size == 0`. |
 | btrfs multi-device RAID | `fs/btrfs/volumes.c` | ✅ | `BtrfsDeviceSet` in ffs-btrfs with multi-device read dispatch and stripe fallback. `read_logical()` resolves via `map_logical_to_stripes` with RAID0/1/5/6/10/DUP support. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies RAID0/RAID5/RAID6 stripe dispatch, RAID10 mirror-stripe dispatch, DUP mirror dispatch, and RAID1 mirror fallback, while `crates/ffs-ondisk/src/btrfs.rs` now adds metamorphic coverage for stripe translation covariance and chunk-order permutation invariance. |
 | btrfs send/receive streams | `fs/btrfs/send.c` | ✅ | `parse_send_stream()` in ffs-btrfs parses the btrfs send stream format (magic, version, 22 command types, attribute TLV encoding). Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` verifies multi-command TLV parsing plus unknown-command fallback to `Unspec` while preserving attributes, and `crates/ffs-harness/tests/btrfs_kernel_reference.rs` now differentially compares FrankenFS normalization against upstream `btrfs receive --dump` on a CRC-valid synthetic send stream. |
 | btrfs chunk tree walking | `fs/btrfs/volumes.c` | ✅ | `walk_chunk_tree()` in ffs-btrfs walks chunk tree beyond sys_chunk_array for complete chunk map. `parse_chunk_item()` parses individual chunk items. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies chunk-tree discovery extends the bootstrap chunk map and returns sorted parsed entries. |
